finding: next --claim reported an owner mismatch as 'Task ATM-GOV-0195 is claimed by codex-governance-optimizer, not codex-governance-optimizer', which is semantically contradictory. The true mismatch was lane-id ownership.
expectedBehavior: Owner mismatch diagnostics should distinguish actor mismatch from lane-session mismatch and avoid saying the same actor is not itself.
evidenceOrRepro: node atm.mjs next --claim --actor codex-governance-optimizer --prompt "Claim batch queue head ATM-GOV-0195..." --json failed with ATM_TASK_CLAIM_OWNER_MISMATCH. data.currentActor and data.actorId were both codex-governance-optimizer; holdingLaneSessionId and requestedLaneSessionId differed.
followUp: Rename the diagnostic or message for ownershipMode=lane-id, include the currently required ATM_LANE_SESSION_ID/adopt command, and add a snapshot test for same-actor lane mismatch.
